Details: In one study, the effect of aeration on mercury removal was studied.  The study indicated that aeration alone is not effective (0 percent removal) at removing Hg(2).  After the addition of a reducing agent (stannous chloride) converting Hg(2) to Hg(0), mercury removal was greater than 99 percent. Aeration, however, is not a BAT for mercury.  Air permitting may also be necessary when releasing mercury into the atmosphere.
